label: About
title: About Komachi Browser Lantern Habitat
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 27 recent saved-signal files and 16 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around Komachi, by Meitei and @andrewjiang: Generative art golf is my favorite corner of X make the most beautiful digital..., plus 7 more. A source image from f4.bcbits.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Komachi Browser Lantern Habitat: A quiet image-led frontpage as a small centered ritual habitat suspended inside deep charcoal negative space. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against large aged parchment-glass central panel, mossy lower habitat and wet pool, and central concentric water ripple.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
